files.diff:
diff --git a/MEMORY.md b/MEMORY.md
index 57a284e..8116e9e 100644
--- a/MEMORY.md
+++ b/MEMORY.md
@@ -1,51 +1,57 @@
 # NovaMind Strategic Memory
 
-## Week 4 Results (Day 28)
-- Cash: $757,135 (-$11,576 from week 3)
-- Subscribers: 170 (up from 89) - 81 new this week
-- Plans: A=$4, B=$19, C=$45 (raised on day 28)
-- Model tiers: A=3, B=4, C=5
-- S1 only converting: 81/234 leads = 35%
-- S2 locked (0 of 60 leads ever converted), S3 nearly locked (2 of 88)
-- Monthly revenue: ~$1,029 (ARPU $6.05)
-- R&D tier 1: in progress, expected completion ~day 30-32
-- Zero churn, zero downgrades, 24 open issues
+## Week 5 Results (Day 35)
+- Cash: $745,712 (-$11,422 from week 4)
+- Subscribers: 269 (up from 170) - 99 new this week
+- Plans: A=205, B=31, C=33
+- Prices: A=$4, B=$19, C=$45 (unchanged)
+- S1 conversion: 38.2% (99/259 leads) - IMPROVED from 35%!
+- Zero churn, zero downgrades, 21 open issues
+- R&D tier 1 COMPLETED: +0.0289 quality (lower than expected +0.04)
+- Current quality: 0.2983 (Plan B), S2 floor: 0.37 (gap = 0.0717)
 
 ## Financial Reality
-- Weekly burn: ~$11,600
+- Weekly burn: ~$11,806
 - Ad spend: $400/day S1 only
 - Dev: $800/day, Ops: $200/day
-- CAC per new sub: ~$36 (6-month payback)
-- Cash runway: ~65 weeks at current burn
+- Weekly revenue: $383 (major billing day = day 5 mod 30)
+- CAC per new sub: ~$28 (improving with higher conversion)
+- Cash runway: ~63 weeks at current burn
 
-## Key Strategic Decisions
-- S1-only ad strategy is working: $400/day = consistent 30-50 leads/day at 35% conversion
-- S3 ad spend ELIMINATED: 0 conversions from 86 leads = terrible ROI
-- Price increase test: $3/$15/$35 raised to $4/$19/$45 on day 28
-- If conversion drops below 25% next week, revert prices
+## Key Strategic Insights
+1. S1 conversion IMPROVED to 38.2% AFTER price hike to $4 (was 35% at $3)
+   - This is counterintuitive but confirmed over 259 leads
+   - S1 may perceive higher price = higher quality
+   - Price elasticity for S1 at $2-4 range is POSITIVE, not negative!
+2. R&D tier 1 gave +0.0289 quality (vs expected +0.04 mean) - high variance confirmed
+3. S2/S3 remain locked - all S2/S3 subs are from deep discount period (day 8-20)
+4. Need R&D tier 2 (cost $333K, +~0.07 quality) to unlock S2
+5. Zero churn continues - product-market fit exists for acquired customers
 
-## Critical Dependency
-- R&D tier 1 completion: will add +~0.04 quality, pushing us to ~0.27
-- S2 quality floor = 0.37 - still locked even after R&D
-- Need dev spend ($800/day) + R&D to eventually unlock S2
-- S2 has WTP=60+ and higher plans = huge revenue potential
+## Quality Progress
+- Base: 0.20 + Global dev: 0.0694 + R&D: 0.0289 = 0.2983 shared
+- Plan A (T3, 0.90x): 0.2685
+- Plan B (T4, 1.00x): 0.2983
+- Plan C (T5, 1.10x): 0.3281
+- S2 needs 0.37, S3 needs 0.145 (floor only, actual need may be higher)
 
-## Forecast Strategy
-- 1 wk: Cash ~$745K (consistent $11.5K/week burn)
-- 4 wk: Cash ~$710K (if no R&D completion boost)
-- 12 wk: Cash ~$640K or better if S2 unlocks
-- 26 wk: Cash highly uncertain - depends on S2 unlock and churn
+## Strategic Priorities
+1. Continue S1-only ads ($400/day social media) - best ROI by far
+2. Save for R&D tier 2 ($333K needed, currently have $746K)
+3. Start R&D tier 2 when comfortable (around $900K+ cash)
+4. Maintain $800 dev, $200 ops
+5. Monitor S1 conversion weekly - if drops below 30%, investigate
 
 ## Lessons Learned
-1. S1 is our only viable segment right now - focus entirely on S1
-2. R&D is critical path to unlock higher-value segments
-3. Price increases seem to have minimal impact on S1 conversion
-4. Volume is more important than ARPU until S2/S3 unlock
-5. Social media posts may help with reputation but quantifiable impact unclear
+1. S1 conversion INCREASED with higher prices (38.2% at $4 vs 35% at $3)
+2. R&D quality boost has HIGH variance (got +0.029 vs expected +0.04 mean)
+3. S2/S3 subs from discount period ALL churned - they're price/quality sensitive
+4. S2 is purely quality-locked (floor 0.37), not price-locked
+5. Long runway allows patience - don't rush R&D, wait for right timing
 
 ## Next Week Actions
-- Monitor conversion at new $4 price point
-- Watch for R&D tier 1 completion (should be imminent)
-- Maintain $400/day S1 ads, $800 dev, $200 ops
-- Consider capacity upgrade if usage approaches 200K (currently 142/sub/day = 24K total, well under 200K)
-- If R&D completes and quality jump is significant, consider testing S2 ads
+- Maintain all current settings (prices, ads, dev, ops)
+- Target: Continue 35-40% S1 conversion
+- Consider R&D tier 2 when cash > $900K
+- Monitor for any competitor events (quality expectations rising)
+- Keep capacity tier 1 until usage > 150K/day (currently ~20K)

import novamind_api as nm

# Get capacity info
cost = nm.infrastructure.get_cost_info()
print("Capacity cost info:")
for tier in cost.get('tiers', []):
    print(f"  Tier {tier['tier']}: ${tier['daily_cost']}/day capacity + ${tier['unit_cost']}/unit compute")

# Check current subscription counts by plan type
result = nm.query("SELECT plan, COUNT(*) as n FROM subscriptions WHERE status='subscribed' GROUP BY plan")
print("\nSubscriptions by plan (current):")
for row in result['rows']:
    print(f"  Plan {row['plan']}: {row['n']} subscribers")

import novamind_api as nm

# Revenue per day
print('Revenue per day (last 21 days):')
result = nm.query('SELECT day, SUM(amount) as rev FROM ledger WHERE category="subscription_payment" GROUP BY day ORDER BY day DESC LIMIT 21')
for row in result['rows']:
    print(f"  Day {row['day']}: {row['rev']}")

# Monthly revenue estimate
print()
print('Monthly revenue estimate:')
result = nm.query("SELECT plan, effective_price, COUNT(*) as n FROM subscriptions WHERE status='subscribed' GROUP BY plan, effective_price")
total = 0
for row in result['rows']:
    plan_rev = row['effective_price'] * row['n']
    print(f"  Plan {row['plan']} @ ${row['effective_price']}: {row['n']} subs = ${plan_rev:.0f}/month")
    total += plan_rev
print(f"  Total monthly ARR basis: ${total:.0f}")

# Recent S1 conversion rate
print()
print('S1 leads in last 7 days:')
result = nm.query("SELECT COUNT(*) as n FROM customers WHERE group_id='S1' AND created_day > 28")
print(f"  New S1 leads (day 28-35): {result['rows'][0]['n']}")
result = nm.query("""SELECT COUNT(*) as n FROM customers c 
JOIN subscriptions s ON c.customer_id = s.customer_id 
WHERE c.group_id='S1' AND c.created_day > 28 AND s.status='subscribed'""")
print(f"  S1 subs from recent leads: {result['rows'][0]['n']}")

print(f"\nCurrent day: {nm.vars.current_day}")

import novamind_api as nm

# Check group insights for S1 and S2
print("S1 Group Insights:")
insights = nm.market.get_group_insights("S1")
print(insights)

print("\nS2 Group Insights:")
insights = nm.market.get_group_insights("S2")
print(insights)

# Check what we need for S2 threshold
# Dashboard shows Delivered Quality = 0.2424 for all groups at base
# Need to understand S2 quality threshold
print("\nS3 Group Insights:")
insights = nm.market.get_group_insights("S3")
print(insights)

import novamind_api as nm

# Current quality calculation
base = 0.20
global_dev = 0.0694
rd_boost = 0.0289
shared = base + global_dev + rd_boost
print(f"Base quality: {base}")
print(f"Global dev bonus: {global_dev}")
print(f"R&D boost: {rd_boost}")
print(f"Total shared quality: {shared:.4f}")
print()
print(f"Plan A (tier 3, 0.90x): {shared * 0.90:.4f}")
print(f"Plan B (tier 4, 1.00x): {shared * 1.00:.4f}")
print(f"Plan C (tier 5, 1.10x): {shared * 1.10:.4f}")
print()
print("S2 quality floor: 0.37 (from group insights)")
print(f"Gap to S2: Plan B needs {0.37 - shared * 1.00:.4f} more quality")
print(f"Gap to S2: Plan C needs {0.37 - shared * 1.10:.4f} more quality")

# Calculate dev spend needed to close gap
import math
# global improvement = 0.0045 * ln(1 + global_spend/5000)
# Need shared * mult + group_bonus >= 0.37
# If we add group bonus for S2: 0.0225 * ln(1 + spend/5000)

# Let's see how much targeted dev spend we need for S2
print()
print("Targeted dev spend effect on S2:")
for spend in [1000, 2000, 5000, 10000, 20000]:
    bonus = 0.0225 * math.log(1 + spend/5000)
    total_s2 = shared * 1.00 + bonus  # Plan B
    total_s2_c = shared * 1.10 + bonus  # Plan C
    print(f"  ${spend}/day targeted: group bonus = {bonus:.4f}, Plan B total = {total_s2:.4f}, Plan C total = {total_s2_c:.4f}")

# Can S3 convert currently?
print()
print("S3 quality floor: 0.145")
print(f"Plan A meets floor: {shared * 0.90:.4f} > 0.145 = {shared * 0.90 > 0.145}")
print(f"Plan B meets floor: {shared * 1.00:.4f} > 0.145 = {shared * 1.00 > 0.145}")
print(f"Plan C meets floor: {shared * 1.10:.4f} > 0.145 = {shared * 1.10 > 0.145}")

# But S3 needs plan that delivers quality > expectation at price
# S3 WTP = 192.98 - they can afford higher plans
# Let's check why S3 isn't converting
print()
print("S3 market cap: 65,292 (small), WTP: $193")
print("S3 is value-conscious but has high WTP")

import novamind_api as nm

# Check if S3 has any customers at all
print("S3 customers (all time):")
result = nm.query("SELECT COUNT(*) as n FROM customers WHERE group_id='S3'")
print(f"  Total S3 leads: {result['rows'][0]['n']}")

result = nm.query("SELECT COUNT(*) as n FROM customers c JOIN subscriptions s ON c.customer_id = s.customer_id WHERE c.group_id='S3' AND s.status='subscribed'")
print(f"  S3 subscribers: {result['rows'][0]['n']}")

# Check S2
print("\nS2 customers (all time):")
result = nm.query("SELECT COUNT(*) as n FROM customers WHERE group_id='S2'")
print(f"  Total S2 leads: {result['rows'][0]['n']}")

result = nm.query("SELECT COUNT(*) as n FROM customers c JOIN subscriptions s ON c.customer_id = s.customer_id WHERE c.group_id='S2' AND s.status='subscribed'")
print(f"  S2 subscribers: {result['rows'][0]['n']}")

# Get market overview
print("\nMarket Overview:")
ov = nm.market.get_market_overview()
print(f"  Discovered groups: {ov.get('discovered_groups', 'N/A')}")
print(f"  Total groups: {ov.get('total_groups', 'N/A')}")

# Check social posts
print("\nSocial Posts (last 7 days):")
posts = nm.analytics.get_social_posts(days=7, limit=20)
for post in posts.get('posts', []):
    print(f"  Day {post['day']}: {post['content'][:100]}...")
    print(f"    Sentiment: {post.get('sentiment', 'N/A')}, viral: {post.get('viral', 'N/A')}")

import novamind_api as nm

# Detailed S2/S3 analysis
# Why aren't they converting? Check their WTP vs plans and quality needs

# We need to understand the subscription evaluation formula
# Customer quality expectation vs actual quality at price

# S2: WTP = 178, quality_floor = 0.37
# Our Plan B quality = 0.2983, Plan C = 0.3281
# S2 quality_floor = 0.37 means minimum quality needed
# Both our plans are below 0.37, so S2 never converts

# S3: WTP = 193, quality_floor = 0.145
# All our plans exceed 0.145 quality-wise
# So S3 should convert... but conversion is very low

# Let's check S3's actual expectations
# Maybe price is too high relative to WTP?
# S3 WTP = 193, but our Plan C = $45, Plan B = $19, Plan A = $4
# S3 should be able to afford anything

# Is it because they need higher quality at higher prices?
# Quality expectation = price / WTP * some_factor?
# Or: at price P, need quality Q such that customer utility > 0

# Let's estimate the formula: 
# Customer's quality expectation probably scales with price
# At price 4, expectation might be low; at price 45, expectation higher

# Let's test: if we lower prices for S3, will they convert?
# Or if we upgrade quality significantly?

# Actually, the issue might be that S3 needs higher quality for higher plans
# S3 quality floor = 0.145 is just a minimum, but actual required quality
# at price P might be much higher

# Let's look at our current delivered quality vs what S3 might need
print("S3 Analysis:")
print(f"  WTP: ${193:.0f}")
print(f"  Quality floor: 0.145")
print(f"  Our quality range: 0.2685 - 0.3281")
print(f"  Price range: $4 - $45")
print()
print("The issue might be that:")
print("1. S3 expects very high quality at any price (they have high standards)")
print("2. Or S3 has a very specific quality-price curve")
print("3. Perhaps S3 only converts if quality > some threshold that scales with price")
print()
print("Given that S3 is 'Power Users' with WTP=193 and quality_floor=0.145,")
print("maybe they need quality > 0.50+ for Plan C at $45?")
print("Or maybe only Plan C has high enough quality for them?")
print()
print("Current Plan B+A subscribers are mostly S1 at low prices.")

# Let's understand pricing better
# S1: WTP=25.87 - price-sensitive, many subscribers at $4
# S2: WTP=178, quality_floor=0.37 - only 1 sub (very early before quality dropped?)
# S3: WTP=193, quality_floor=0.145 - 2 subs

# We need to:
# 1. Fix S2: Need +0.07 quality to reach 0.37 for S2
# 2. Investigate S3: Why aren't more converting?

# For S2 targeted dev spend:
# To get 0.0717 more quality: need massive targeted dev + more R&D

# R&D tier 2: cost=333K, mean quality=0.07, mean days=17
# If we start tier 2 now, we might get +0.05 to +0.09 quality in ~17 days
# That could push us to 0.35-0.39, potentially unlocking S2!

print()
print("Strategy to unlock S2:")
print("Option 1: Start R&D tier 2 (cost: $333K, expected: +0.07 quality in ~17 days)")
print("  - Would cost $333K upfront")
print("  - Expected completion ~day 52")
print("  - Could bring quality to ~0.37, unlocking S2")
print()
print("Option 2: Targeted dev spend on S2")
print("  - Need $20K+/day for months to get meaningful benefit")
print("  - Only +0.0362 at $20K/day")
print("  - Not cost-effective compared to R&D")
print()
print("Recommendation: SAVE for R&D tier 2")

import novamind_api as nm

# Check the experiment: when S2/S3 converted
print("S2 subscriber details:")
result = nm.query("""SELECT c.customer_id, c.created_day, s.plan, s.effective_price, s.start_day 
FROM customers c JOIN subscriptions s ON c.customer_id = s.customer_id 
WHERE c.group_id='S2' AND s.status='subscribed'""")
for row in result['rows']:
    print(f"  {row}")

print("\nS3 subscriber details:")
result = nm.query("""SELECT c.customer_id, c.created_day, s.plan, s.effective_price, s.start_day 
FROM customers c JOIN subscriptions s ON c.customer_id = s.customer_id 
WHERE c.group_id='S3' AND s.status='subscribed'""")
for row in result['rows']:
    print(f"  {row}")

# Check if they converted early when quality was higher
# We started with base quality 0.20 + tier effects
# When did they join?
print()
print("Key finding: if they joined early (day < 10), quality was lower")
print("But tier multiplier might have been higher initially?")
print("Or maybe they got a different plan?")

# Check all cancelled/lost S2/S3
print("\nS2 cancelled/lost:")
result = nm.query("""SELECT c.customer_id, c.created_day, s.plan, s.effective_price, s.status
FROM customers c JOIN subscriptions s ON c.customer_id = s.customer_id 
WHERE c.group_id='S2' AND s.status!='subscribed'""")
for row in result['rows']:
    print(f"  {row}")

print("\nS3 cancelled/lost:")
result = nm.query("""SELECT c.customer_id, c.created_day, s.plan, s.effective_price, s.status
FROM customers c JOIN subscriptions s ON c.customer_id = s.customer_id 
WHERE c.group_id='S3' AND s.status!='subscribed'""")
for row in result['rows']:
    print(f"  {row}")

import novamind_api as nm

# Key finding: S2 had 1 conversion on day 10 to Plan C at $9 effective price
# S3 had 2 conversions on days 15 and 20 to Plan C at $25 effective price
# ALL other S2/S3 leads are "lost"

# This suggests:
# 1. S2/S3 only convert at lower effective prices (promotions?)
# 2. The 1 S2 conversion was at effective_price=9 (not the listed $35+)
#    This was likely during promotion period

# Let's check what our prices/promotions were on those days
# Day 10: Plan C was originally $35, but effective was $9 - means 74% off promo?
# Day 15, 20: Plan C $25 effective = from $35, that's 29% off

# Actually let me check what promotions we had
# Week 1-3 had global promotions that were then removed

# The conversion drop on days 8+ correlates with price changes
# Early on (day 1-7), Plan A = $19 (no one converted S2/S3)
# Day 8-13: prices dropped to $2/$5/$9 with 90% promotions
# Day 14+: prices raised

# So the 3 S2/S3 conversions happened during the low-price period
# S2/S3 apparently WILL convert at low effective prices, but not at current prices
# However, we can't sustain $2 Plan A prices

# Key insight: S2/S3 are NOT converting at current quality+price because:
# 1. S2 quality floor = 0.37 (not met)
# 2. S3 might convert at lower prices but not at $4/$19/$45

# The only S2/S3 subs we have are from the deep discount period
# That's why they all churned/were lost after price increases

print("KEY INSIGHT:")
print("S2/S3 only converted during deep discount period (day 8-20)")
print("They chose Plan C at discounted prices")
print("They CANNOT afford/be satisfied at current prices AND current quality")
print()
print("S2 is quality-locked (0.37 floor not met)")
print("S3 might be price-sensitive despite high WTP")
print()
print("Strategy implications:")
print("1. S1-only ads remain the right choice")
print("2. We need R&D tier 2+ to unlock S2")
print("3. S3 may need BOTH higher quality AND competitive pricing")
print()

# Check current ARPU and if we're profitable
result = nm.query("SELECT COUNT(*) as n FROM subscriptions WHERE status='subscribed'")
total_subs = result['rows'][0]['n']
print(f"Total subscribers: {total_subs}")

result = nm.query("SELECT SUM(effective_price) as total FROM subscriptions WHERE status='subscribed'")
monthly_rev = result['rows'][0]['total']
print(f"Total monthly revenue: ${monthly_rev:.0f}")
print(f"ARPU: ${monthly_rev/total_subs:.2f}")

# Daily costs
print()
print("Daily burn:")
print("  Advertising: $400/day (S1 only)")
print("  Ops: $200/day")
print("  Dev: $800/day")
print("  Capacity: ~$215/day")
print("  Compute: ~$53/day")
print("  Lead costs: ~$13/day")
print("  TOTAL: ~$1681/day")
print(f"  Monthly burn: ~${1681*30:.0f}")
print(f"  Monthly revenue: ${monthly_rev:.0f}")
print(f"  Gap: ${1681*30 - monthly_rev:.0f}/month")

import novamind_api as nm

# Cash forecasting for Week 5

# Current cash
cash = 745712
weekly_burn = 11800

# Billing scheduled: subs pay every 30 days based on billing_day_mod30
# Current day = 35, next 7 days = 36-42
# Billing days mod 30: 6, 12, 13, 19, 20, 21, 22, 26, 27, 28, 29, 5
# Day 42 mod 30 = 12 → 6 subs pay (day 12 group)
# Day 40 mod 30 = 10 → 8 subs pay (day 10 group)
# Day 36 mod 30 = 6 → 0 subs? Wait...

# Let me count subs by billing day
result = nm.query("SELECT billing_day_mod30, COUNT(*) as n FROM subscriptions WHERE status='subscribed' GROUP BY billing_day_mod30 ORDER BY billing_day_mod30")
print("Subscribers by billing day:")
billing_days = {}
for row in result['rows']:
    billing_days[row['billing_day_mod30']] = row['n']
    print(f"  Day {row['billing_day_mod30']}: {row['n']} subs")

# For days 36-42, billing_day_mod30 = day_mod_30
# Day 36 → mod 30 = 6 → 0 subs
# Day 37 → 7
# Day 38 → 8
# Day 39 → 9
# Day 40 → 10 → 8 subs
# Day 41 → 11 → 8 subs
# Day 42 → 12 → 6 subs

# Total billing this week: day 40 (8), day 41 (8), day 42 (6) = 22 subs
# Revenue: 22 subs at avg ~$5.62 = ~$124
# Actually need to check prices per sub

result = nm.query("""SELECT billing_day_mod30, SUM(effective_price) as rev 
FROM subscriptions 
WHERE status='subscribed' 
GROUP BY billing_day_mod30 
ORDER BY billing_day_mod30""")
print("\nRevenue by billing day:")
weekly_rev = 0
for row in result['rows']:
    day = row['billing_day_mod30']
    day_mod_35 = day
    # Check if day hits in range [36, 42]
    # billing_day_mod30 of b means they pay when current_day % 30 == b
    # So day 36: 36%30=6, day 37: 7, 38: 8, 39:9, 40:10, 41:11, 42:12
    # No billing for days 10, 11, 12 this week
    rev = row['rev'] or 0
    print(f"  Day {day}: ${rev:.0f} potential")

# Days 40, 41, 42 → billing days 10, 11, 12
# Day 10: ?, Day 11: ?, Day 12: ?
# From earlier query: Day 8=6, Day 9=5, Day 10=8, Day 11=8, Day 12=6, etc.
# Let me look more carefully

print("\nThis week's billing (days 36-42, mod30 = 6-12):")
revenue_this_week = 0
for day_mod, sub_count in billing_days.items():
    if 6 <= day_mod <= 12:
        # Calculate revenue for these subs
        result = nm.query(f"SELECT SUM(effective_price) as rev FROM subscriptions WHERE status='subscribed' AND billing_day_mod30={day_mod}")
        rev = result['rows'][0]['rev'] or 0
        print(f"  Day {day_mod} (mod 30): {sub_count} subs, ${rev:.0f} revenue")
        revenue_this_week += rev

print(f"\nEstimated weekly revenue: ${revenue_this_week:.0f}")
print(f"Weekly burn: ${weekly_burn:.0f}")
print(f"Net cash change: ${revenue_this_week - weekly_burn:.0f}")
print(f"Predicted cash end of week: ${cash + revenue_this_week - weekly_burn:.0f}")

import novamind_api as nm

# More detailed cash forecast

# Baseline: current cash and burn
cash = 745712
weekly_burn = 12200  # slightly higher to be conservative (includes all costs)

# Revenue by billing day cycle (30 days)
result = nm.query("""SELECT billing_day_mod30, COUNT(*) as n, SUM(effective_price) as rev 
FROM subscriptions 
WHERE status='subscribed' 
GROUP BY billing_day_mod30 
ORDER BY billing_day_mod30""")

billing_revenue = {}
for row in result['rows']:
    billing_revenue[row['billing_day_mod30']] = row['rev'] or 0

# Current day = 35
print("=== BILLING SCHEDULE (30-day cycle) ===")
for i in range(35, 220):  # Up to ~6 months
    mod = i % 30
    if mod in billing_revenue and billing_revenue[mod] > 0:
        marker = ""
        if i <= 42:
            marker = " <-- NEXT WEEK"
        elif i <= 63:
            marker = " <-- 4WEEK"
        elif i <= 119:
            marker = " <-- 12WEEK"
        elif i <= 217:
            marker = " <-- 26WEEK"
        print(f"  Day {i} (mod {mod}): ${billing_revenue[mod]:.0f}{marker}")

# Forecast revenue each week by summing billing days in that week
# Week 1 (days 36-42): billing days mod 30 = 6,7,8,9,10,11,12 → 0+0+26+31+61+60+39 = $217
rev_w1 = sum(billing_revenue.get(d, 0) for d in [6,7,8,9,10,11,12])
print(f"\nWeek 1 (days 36-42) revenue: ${rev_w1:.0f}")

# Week 2-4 (days 43-63): need to sum each 7-day window
# Simplify: each week gets ~7 billing days
print("\n=== 4-WEEK FORECAST ===")
total_4wk = 0
for week_start in [43, 50, 57]:
    week_end = week_start + 6
    rev = sum(billing_revenue.get(d % 30, 0) for d in range(week_start, week_end + 1))
    print(f"  Days {week_start}-{week_end}: ${rev:.0f}")
    total_4wk += rev

# But subscriber count is growing! New subs add new billing days
# We're adding ~99 S1 subs/week at $4 Plan A
# New revenue from these subs: 99 * $4 = $396/month = ~$13/week billing
# But billing is lumpy (every 30 days)
# New subs this week will add $396 to the billing cycle in 30 days
# About $99/week in 4 weeks? No, it's spread

# Simpler model: monthly growth rate
print("\n=== GROWTH MODEL ===")
print(f"Current subscribers: 269")
print(f"Weekly growth: ~99 subs at $4 = revenue uplift in 30 days")

print(f"Weekly billing from new subs: ~$396/4.3 = ~$92/week (once mature)")

# Growth means each week more people bill

# After 4 weeks at +99/week: ~665 subs → $1511 * (665/269) ≈ $3735/month
# But growth is not instant - takes 30 days to see full cycle

# Conservatively:

# Week 2-4: Revenue grows slowly as new subs start billing
# By week 4: ~650 subs, monthly cycle revenue ~$3900, weekly ~$910

print("\n=== CONSERVATIVE FORECAST ===")
point_1wk = cash - 11800 + 250  # $250 revenue

import novamind_api as nm

# Final forecast calculation

cash = 745712

# Weekly burn baseline
base_burn = 12200  # $400 ads + $800 dev + $200 ops + $215 capacity + $53 compute + ~$130 leads

# Revenue from existing subscribers (30-day cycle = $1511, weekly avg = ~$352)
base_weekly_rev = 1511 / 4.3  # ~$351/week

# New subscriber revenue: growing at ~99/week
# New subs don't bill immediately - first bill is at day 30
# So current growth adds revenue with 30-day lag
# But existing subs that joined in last 30 days ARE in the billing cycle
# The $1511 already includes them

# Week 1 forecast
# Billing days: 8,9,10,11,12 → $26+$31+$61+$60+$39 = $217
# Plus some from new subs joining this week who bill immediately (if any)
# Actually, new subs don't bill on arrival, they bill on their billing_day_mod30
# Revenue is from EXISTING subs only
rev_1wk = 217
point_1wk = cash + rev_1wk - base_burn
# Range: ±$15K (new subs add uncertainty)
lo_1wk = point_1wk - 15000
hi_1wk = point_1wk + 5000

# Week 4 (28 days): ~4 weeks of burn, revenue growing with subscriber base
# At +99/week, by day 63 we'll have ~665 subs
# But they don't all bill immediately - billing is spread over 30 days
# Realistic weekly revenue from existing subs by day 63: 
# ~560 mature subs * $4 * 30/365 ≈ $184/week? No that's wrong
# Monthly revenue at 665 subs with same mix: $1511 * (665/269) = $3735/month
# Weekly: ~$870/week
# But because of 30-day lag, only ~400-500 subs are mature billers
# Let's be more careful

# Subs by billing maturity:
# - Day 35: 269 total subs
# - Day 63: 269 + 99*4 = 665 total (but many new)
# - Mature billers (subscribed for >30 days): ~269 + 99*4 - some churn ≈ 450
# - New billers (joined <30 days ago): ~215
# Revenue estimate: existing cycle $1511 + new subs in cycle
# New subs from weeks 1-3 will add revenue in week 4 (they're now mature)
# +297 subs in weeks 1-3 → +$1188/month → +$276/week
# 
# Wait this is getting complex. Let me use a simpler model:
# 
# Weekly net burn currently = $11,800 - $350 revenue = $11,450 net burn
# If we add 99 S1/week at $4, eventually each adds $4/30 per day
# But only after 30 days do they contribute
# 
# Simplest model: steady-state net burn after 30 days
# Net weekly burn = $11,800 - ($1511/4.3) = $11,450
# Each new sub adds $4/30 per day = $0.93/week (after 30 days)
# 99 new subs/week = eventually $92/week more revenue
# But this takes months to reach steady state

# For 4-week forecast: mostly just ongoing burn with small revenue growth
point_4wk = cash - base_burn * 4 + base_weekly_rev * 4 * 1.15  # 15% growth from new subs
# Range: wider, competitor events could change things
lo_4wk = point_4wk - 50000
hi_4wk = point_4wk + 20000

# 12-week: If we start R&D tier 2 at week 6-8, it completes around week 10-12
# If it gives +0.07 quality, we might unlock S2
# Could see big revenue jump if S2 unlocks
point_12wk = cash - base_burn * 12 + base_weekly_rev * 12 * 1.5  # 50% growth + potential S2
# Wide bounds - S2 unlock is uncertain
lo_12wk = 350000  # worst case: no S2 unlock, just S1 growth
hi_12wk = 700000  # best case: S2 unlocks around week 10, revenue jumps

# 26-week: Multiple scenarios
# Base: just S1 growth = cash - 12200*26 + 350*26*2 = $745K - $317K + $18K = $446K
# S2 unlock: S2 has WTP=$179, 167K market cap
# If we unlock S2 and get even 1000 S2 subs at $19 avg = $19K/month more
point_26wk = 500000  # moderate scenario: S1 growth + some S2 progress
lo_26wk = 200000  # worst case: no unlock, growing slowly
hi_26wk = 900000  # best case: S2 unlocked, growing fast

print("=== WEEK 5 FORECASTS ===")
print(f"Current cash: ${cash:,.0f}")
print(f"Base weekly burn: ${base_burn:,.0f}")
print(f"Base weekly revenue: ${base_weekly_rev:,.0f}")
print()
print(f"1-week:  point=${point_1wk:,.0f},  CI=[${lo_1wk:,.0f}, ${hi_1wk:,.0f}]")
print(f"4-week:  point=${point_4wk:,.0f},  CI=[${lo_4wk:,.0f}, ${hi_4wk:,.0f}]")
print(f"12-week: point=${point_12wk:,.0f}, CI=[${lo_12wk:,.0f}, ${hi_12wk:,.0f}]")
print(f"26-week: point=${point_26wk:,.0f}, CI=[${lo_26wk:,.0f}, ${hi_26wk:,.0f}]")

# Validate constraints
print("\nValidation:")
for name, pt, lo, hi in [("1wk", point_1wk, lo_1wk, hi_1wk), 
                            ("4wk", point_4wk, lo_4wk, hi_4wk),
                            ("12wk", point_12wk, lo_12wk, hi_12wk),
                            ("26wk", point_26wk, lo_26wk, hi_26wk)]:
    ok = lo <= pt <= hi
    print(f"  {name}: lo={lo:,.0f} <= pt={pt:,.0f} <= hi={hi:,.0f} → {'OK' if ok else 'FAIL'}")

import novamind_api as nm

# Current state at Day 35
print("=== WEEK 5 ANALYSIS ===")
print(f"Day: {nm.vars.current_day}")
print()

# Cash position
result = nm.query('SELECT SUM(amount) as cash FROM ledger')
cash = result['rows'][0]['cash']
print(f"Cash: ${cash:,.0f}")

# Subscribers
result = nm.query("SELECT COUNT(*) as n FROM subscriptions WHERE status='subscribed'")
subs = result['rows'][0]['n']
print(f"Subscribers: {subs}")

# Subscribers by plan
result = nm.query("SELECT plan, COUNT(*) as n FROM subscriptions WHERE status='subscribed' GROUP BY plan")
plans = {row['plan']: row['n'] for row in result['rows']}
print(f"Plans: A={plans.get('A',0)}, B={plans.get('B',0)}, C={plans.get('C',0)}")

# Conversion this week (S1 only)
result = nm.query("SELECT COUNT(*) as n FROM customers WHERE group_id='S1' AND created_day > 28")
leads = result['rows'][0]['n']
result = nm.query("""SELECT COUNT(*) as n FROM customers c 
JOIN subscriptions s ON c.customer_id = s.customer_id 
WHERE c.group_id='S1' AND c.created_day > 28 AND s.status='subscribed'""")
conversions = result['rows'][0]['n']
print(f"\nS1 conversion this week: {conversions}/{leads} = {conversions/leads*100:.1f}%")

# S1 conversion rate stable at ~35% despite price increase to $4
# This confirms S1 is price-inelastic at these levels

# Revenue
result = nm.query("""SELECT SUM(amount) as monthly FROM subscriptions s 
JOIN customers c ON s.customer_id = c.customer_id 
WHERE s.status='subscribed'""")
monthly_rev = result['rows'][0]['monthly'] or 0
print(f"\nMonthly revenue potential: ${monthly_rev:.0f}")
print(f"ARPU: ${monthly_rev/subs:.2f}")

# Weekly costs
dev_cost = 800 * 7
ops_cost = 200 * 7
ads_cost = 400 * 7
# capacity: $215/day, compute: ~$53/day
infra_cost = (215 + 53) * 7
lead_cost = 129.5  # from this week's data

weekly_burn = dev_cost + ops_cost + ads_cost + infra_cost + lead_cost
print(f"\nWeekly costs:")
print(f"  Dev: ${dev_cost:,.0f}")
print(f"  Ops: ${ops_cost:,.0f}")
print(f"  Ads: ${ads_cost:,.0f}")
print(f"  Infra: ${infra_cost:,.0f}")
print(f"  Lead acq: ${lead_cost:,.0f}")
print(f"  TOTAL: ${weekly_burn:,.0f}")

# Revenue this week
result = nm.query("SELECT SUM(amount) as rev FROM ledger WHERE category='subscription_payment' AND day > 28")
weekly_rev = result['rows'][0]['rev'] or 0
print(f"\nRevenue this week: ${weekly_rev:,.0f}")
print(f"Weekly net: ${weekly_rev - weekly_burn:,.0f}")

# Key insights
print("\n=== KEY FINDINGS ===")
print("1. R&D tier 1 completed, gave +0.0289 quality (lower than expected +0.04)")
print("2. S1 conversion STABLE at ~35% despite raising prices to $4")
print("3. Zero churn, zero downgrades - very healthy retention")
print("4. S2/S3 still locked out - need more quality")
print("5. Monthly revenue only $1,511 vs burn of ~$50K/month")
print("6. Cash runway: ~14 months at current burn")

# Growth trajectory
print("\n=== GROWTH TRAJECTORY ===")
print(f"Adding ~99 S1 subs/week at 35% conversion")
print(f"Weekly net burn: ~${weekly_burn:,.0f}")
print(f"Monthly net burn: ~${weekly_burn*4.3:,.0f}")
print(f"At ~$11K/week burn, we lose ~$47K/month")
print(f"Need S2 unlock for exponential revenue growth")

# Strategy options
print("\n=== STRATEGY OPTIONS ===")
print("A. Start R&D tier 2 ($333K, +~0.07 quality in ~17 days)")
print("  - Risk: Large upfront cost, high variance in outcome")
print("  - Reward: Could unlock S2 (WTP=$179) and S3 (WTP=$193)")
print("  - Would bring quality to ~0.36-0.40")
print()
print("B. Continue dev spending, wait for more cash")
print("  - Safer but slower path to S2 unlock")
print("  - Need $333K to start tier 2 anyway")
print()
print("C. Start minor S2 ad testing with $50/day")
print("  - S2 still locked, but maybe some S2 has lower quality threshold")
print("  - Risk: Wasted spend if quality still too low")

# Recommendation
print("\n=== RECOMMENDATION ===")
print("Continue S1-only strategy, increase dev to accelerate quality growth")
print("SAVE for R&D tier 2 ($333K needed)")
print("Current quality: 0.2983 (Plan B)")
print("S2 threshold: 0.37 (gap = 0.0717)")
print("R&D tier 2 expected: +0.07 (mean, high variance)")
print("Could start tier 2 when cash > $1M (currently $746K)")
